---
title: "Cross-Mode Transitions"
subtitle: "Move seamlessly between Search, Explore, and Evaluate"
---

## Goal

The platform's three modes --- Explore, Search, and Evaluate --- are not isolated silos. At many points in a Search workflow you will want to pivot: evaluate a promising BGC in detail, explore the neighbourhood of a hit in the full catalogue, or track down purchasable strains. This page catalogues every cross-mode action available from Search, explains what each one does, and describes what data carries over.

## When to use this page

Refer to this page when you see an action button in a Search results panel and want to know what it will do, or when you are planning a multi-step analysis that spans modes.

## Cross-mode actions reference

The table below lists all cross-mode transitions available from within Search mode. Each action appears as a button or menu item in the BGC Detail, Assembly Detail, or roster toolbar.

| Action | Where it appears | Target mode | What it does |
|--------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|
| **Find Similar BGCs** | BGC Detail panel, BGC roster context menu | Search | Takes the selected BGC's protein embedding and launches a new Sequence Search using it as the query. Useful for pivoting from one hit to find its relatives. |
| **Evaluate BGC** | BGC Detail panel | Evaluate | Opens the selected BGC in Evaluate mode, generating a comprehensive BGC Report with domain architecture, predicted products, novelty analysis, and MIBiG comparisons. |
| **Evaluate Assembly** | Assembly Detail panel, Assembly roster context menu | Evaluate | Opens the selected assembly in Evaluate mode, generating an Assembly Report with quality metrics, full BGC inventory, taxonomic placement, and culture-collection links. |
| **Browse Similar Assemblies** | Assembly Detail panel | Explore | Switches to Explore mode centred on the selected assembly's neighbourhood in the Assembly Space Map. Assemblies with similar BGC profiles cluster together, letting you discover related organisms. |
| **Find in Purchasable Strains** | Assembly Detail panel, Assembly roster toolbar (batch) | Explore (filtered) | Switches to Explore mode with the Type Strain filter pre-applied and the Assembly Space Map zoomed to the region around the selected assembly. This surfaces culturable organisms that are genomically similar. |
| **Add to Shortlist** | BGC roster star icon, Assembly roster star icon | Stays in current mode | Adds the BGC or assembly to your shortlist. The shortlist persists across all mode switches and can be reviewed from any mode. |

## Step-by-step: common cross-mode workflows

### From a search hit to a full BGC evaluation

1. Run a search (domain, sequence, or chemical) and review the results in the BGC Roster.
2. Click a high-scoring BGC to open its BGC Detail panel.
3. Click **Evaluate BGC** in the detail panel.
4. The platform switches to Evaluate mode and generates a [BGC Report](bgc-report.qmd). The report includes:
   - Complete domain architecture, gene by gene.
   - Predicted natural product structure (if available).
   - Novelty analysis against the MIBiG reference database.
   - Links to homologous BGCs and related literature.
5. From the BGC Report, you can navigate to the parent assembly's evaluation or return to Search.

### From a search hit to exploring its neighbourhood

1. In the BGC Roster, identify a BGC that scored well but belongs to an unfamiliar class or biome.
2. Open the Assembly Detail for its parent assembly (click the assembly accession in the BGC Detail panel).
3. Click **Browse Similar Assemblies**.
4. The platform switches to Explore mode, centred on that assembly in the Assembly Space Map. Nearby points represent assemblies with similar BGC profiles.
5. Pan and zoom the map to discover organisms in the same ecological or taxonomic neighbourhood. This is useful for understanding the broader distribution of your BGC type.

### From a search hit to purchasable strains

1. Star several promising BGCs in the BGC Roster to add them to your shortlist.
2. The Assembly Triad below populates with the parent assemblies of your shortlisted BGCs.
3. In the Assembly Roster, click **Find in Purchasable Strains** (available as a batch action in the toolbar).
4. The platform switches to Explore mode with the **Type Strain** filter active. The Assembly Space Map zooms to the region containing your shortlisted assemblies.
5. The roster now shows only assemblies that correspond to strains available from culture collections, sorted by proximity to your original hits. Each entry includes a direct link to the culture collection catalogue.
6. Select an assembly and click **Evaluate Assembly** to review its full [Assembly Report](assembly-report.qmd) before placing an order.

### Pivoting to find more relatives of a hit

1. In the BGC Detail panel for a search hit, click **Find Similar BGCs**.
2. The platform stays in Search mode but opens a new Sequence Search pre-loaded with the selected BGC's protein sequence as the query.
3. Adjust the similarity threshold if desired and click **Run Query**.
4. The new results show all BGCs in the database that are similar to your original hit --- potentially including BGCs that did not match your first query because they lack the required domains or chemical similarity, but are nonetheless close relatives at the sequence level.

## What data carries over

Understanding what persists across mode transitions helps you work efficiently:

| Data | Persists? | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|
| **Shortlist** | Yes | Your shortlisted BGCs and assemblies are available in every mode. |
| **Active filters** | Partially | When a transition pre-applies a filter (e.g. Type Strain for "Find in Purchasable Strains"), it replaces your current filter set. Other transitions preserve filters where they apply to both modes. |
| **Search query** | No | Your query is specific to Search mode. When you return to Search, the query form retains your last inputs, but navigating to Explore or Evaluate does not carry the query. |
| **Selected BGC/Assembly** | Yes | The item you clicked to trigger the transition becomes the focal item in the target mode. |
| **Sort order** | No | Each mode has its own default sort. The target mode's default applies. |

## Tips

- **Use the shortlist as your bridge.** Star items in Search, then switch to Explore or Evaluate. The shortlist is the most reliable way to carry a set of candidates between modes.
- **Use "Find Similar BGCs" iteratively.** Start with one search, find a strong hit, then pivot with "Find Similar BGCs" to explore its neighbourhood in sequence space. Each iteration can uncover a different slice of diversity.
- **Combine "Browse Similar Assemblies" with biome filters.** After switching to Explore mode, apply a biome filter to see whether your hit's relatives appear in specific environments (e.g. only in marine assemblies, or distributed across soil and freshwater).
- **Return to Search easily.** The mode selector at the top of the dashboard always lets you switch back. Your last query inputs are preserved in the Query tab.

## Related pages

- [Search BGCs Overview](search-overview.qmd) --- the Search interface layout
- [Reading Search Results](search-results.qmd) --- understanding the results before transitioning
- [Explore Assemblies Overview](explore-overview.qmd) --- the Explore mode you may transition into
- [Evaluate Overview](evaluate-overview.qmd) --- the Evaluate mode for deep-dive reports
- [Shortlists](shortlists.qmd) --- managing your saved BGCs and assemblies across modes
